reviews.review_text:
A Really good mouse changer, if u do not trust and think its bad just trust me u wont regret it. I love this it has a lot of changes to your mouse you will love this.    

-Ben

[h1] Good app but somewhat lacking if you only wish for the customization, not the flexibility [/h1]
This is a great mouse customization tool.. BUT i can't recommend this to most people.

The good:
-Quite a variety of choices. you get lots of options but those are mainly for the basic mouse, and usually not for the actions it can display.

-You can change mouse model per app so it's really flexible

-Really interesting default options. the noodle is actually what i had on my pc screen for at least 90 hours. If the bad things didn't occur to me i would recommend this in a heartbeat only for the noodle

-It has some interesting effects, trails, crosshair option and localization that you can place on any design. sadly those were not for me.

-The ability to use presets of old game's designs sure is neat 

The bad:
- (WAS TOLD THIS WAS A BUG TO BE FIXED NEXT UPDATE) REALLY HIGH RESOURCE UTILISATION for actually no reason. I've checked different models (3D, still images, animated), regardless of what i used i got a constant 12% GPU and 6% CPU load. That is crazy for a still image mouse, especially after using other mouse customization apps that do not have such a problem. Wallpaper engine with a 2k animated background and a 1920 animated one runs at 6% GPU with 2% CPU load. (On a gaming laptop that is the difference between 15% fan power and 60% fan power)

-DOES NOT WORK IF THE APP IS OFF. Usually a apps would implement the changes into your windows settings, this app does not and as a consequence you have to keep it on non-stop. This is so the app can change designs per app, it's nice to know but it would be lovely if there was the option to set a "default" design that would work even if the app was off

-Interface is pretty confusing, it's easy to change something without wanting to and find yourself at a loss to do something.

-Can get glitchy sometimes but i rather enjoyed playing with the noodle when it left glitchy traces and so. It's not really common, just when starting some apps or getting an error.

FINAL NOTE: If you just want customization tool that has more options that incorporate all the actions a mouse should display, simpler interface and works even when turned off go for " Mouse Pointer Engine ", it's basically Wallpaper engine. If you are looking for the FLEXIBILITY that i mentioned above this one takes the cake by a mile without a doubt.

Originally bought this just because it showcased Guild Wars 2 in the promo pictures/videos.

I'm forever glad I got it. Always lose track of my mouse in games and this already seems to be massively helping. Was afraid how annoying it was going to be that it'd *constantly* state me as playing it but has the added functionality of "disconnecting" from steam. Also afraid I was going to hate to set the mice *each* time I launch a game or that it'd only work for one game but... It does seem to save profiles for each game.

Workshop support being a thing as well just pushes this even higher since you don't need to download annoying .cur files.

An absolute godsend this is. Ty for making this.
i play geometry dash and when i open the game my mouse disappears, and its really annoying since i have to turn it off then on everytime i play gd.

I can fulfill my childhood dream of using an Old School Runescape Dragon Dagger P++ as a cursor and not get my account hacked. 2007 me would be thrilled. 

10/10 would buy again.

Great little app!!!  Needs a little help with stability.  Mouse pointer disappears and changes colors randomly but still a cool app.  If it was a free app the quirks would be acceptable, but for a paid app it should be better!
One of the best programs you can buy on Steam. Useful for so many games and applications.
NO MORE DEATH, OR AT EAST LESS, WITH A BRIGHT RED POINTER IN THE SNOWS OF GUILDWARS
Helps make the mouse visible without making it super huge. Very customizable.

[h1] If you can't see your chores, you can't clean them! [/h1]

[i]"Losing your cursor in a fight is like losing your child in a supermarket. Irresponsible and completely avoidable."[/i]

[hr][/hr]

[b]The Manager's Perspective:[/b]
I do not have time for games that hide information from me. When I am in the middle of a chaotic battle, I need to know [b]exactly[/b] where my pointer is. I cannot waste three seconds shaking my mouse like an idiot while monsters are ruining my efficiency ratings. 

[h3]  Why YoloMouse is mandatory for a clean desktop: [/h3]
[list]
[*] [b]Absolute Visibility:[/b] You can make the cursor neon pink and three times its normal size. It’s like putting a giant label on everything in the kitchen. Perfect.
[*] [b]No Excuses:[/b] Now Gumball can’t pretend he "didn't see" the button to close the game and go do his homework. It's right there!
[*] [b]Performance Boost:[/b] Precision is key. If you miss your target because your cursor blended into the background, that is a failure of planning.
[/list]

[h3]  A Direct Order [/h3]
If I check your setups and find out you are still squinting at the screen trying to find a tiny grey arrow during a raid, you will be demoted. Use the tools available to maximize your productivity!

[hr][/hr]

[b]Final Verdict:[/b]
It’s not a game, it’s a solution to a logistical problem. It’s simple, it works, and it doesn't talk back. Exactly how I like my software.

[b]Rating: 9.5/10 - Cleaner than a newly waxed floor.[/b]
